extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a is set true only by r3 (IF bradycardia AND immunosuppressed). rec_drug_b is set true only by r2 (IF bradycardia). These touch different outputs, so per-output priority resolution does not let one suppress the other, and no rule ever sets either to false except the default (which only applies when an output is left unset). With bradycardia=true and immunosuppressed=true: r2 fires -> rec_drug_b=true, and r3 fires -> rec_drug_a=true (diabetes can be false; r1 is irrelevant to a and b). Thus rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b are jointly true, violating the mutual-exclusion property.
